texto: I could hardly wait for following chapters, which arrived in dribs and drabs, and I began to feel for all the world like the young T.B. Macaulay walking from London to meet the Cambridge coach bearing the next installment of Waverley novels.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Vernon Sproxton: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “I could hardly wait for following chapters, which arrived in dribs and drabs, and I began to feel…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, books, reading pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reading.
